transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hold on.
Oh, hey, I didn't realize I was on mute.
How's it going?
It's Friday.
It's Halloween.
I'm going to go camera off because I'm in a gas station parking lot.
I'm trying to make it to my niece and nephew's house for Halloween.
That's like they're like three hours away and I didn't realize how early everybody goes trick-or-treating.
So I was like, oh my gosh, have to get in the car at noon.
Oh my gosh, that'll be so much fun now.
Yeah, they're going to be super cute, but I'll just turn my video off because it's a weird background.
Hi, Marylise.
Hi.
How are you?
Good, I'm good, yeah.
I dropped off my kid with his Halloween costume, and he was really excited, actually, like, did not put him in the bus, like, I went.
So I was like, oh my gosh, it must have been so cute.
Like, every kid, I saw a couple buses going by this morning when I was on my walk of, like, kids all dressed up.
It was really cute.
Yeah.
Oh, how fun.
I, there are no kids around me.
Where are you?
Right now, I split my time between Washington State and Tucson.
Okay.
So, like, where I'm at in Tucson, it's, you know, older people.
Probably more than, more than anything.
Yeah, there are no kids.
Okay, so yeah, thanks for being flexible.
It was a little bit crazy yesterday with the launch, so.
Yeah, how'd it go?
It went well, yeah, yeah, it was well, like, yeah, we had a couple of delays last week.
were going to launch last week and we ended up pushing it to this week.
So I wanted to make sure it was focused.
Yeah, that makes a lot of sense.
Cool.
Well, we can be relatively quick today.
I think just, like, re, sort of reiterating the things that we're doing on our end, in that we're going to be generating those two articles.
And Ada and Ife both confirmed, like, when they were generating those outlines, they were not, like, we weren't thinking about tone.
We were just thinking about, like, accuracy and structure.
So I think when we see the articles, hopefully that will, you'll.
See a little bit better reflection of like the BaseTen voice in those.
Yeah, yeah, because I was just like, yeah, they felt so robotic to me.
I was like, I can't even imagine, you know.
So, yeah, I'm looking forward to seeing like the articles and see how it works.
Because, you I've been thinking a lot about this and we are so particular about our blog and our content.
Like part of me is like, can we even get someone external, like do it for us?
And so that's why I was just like, I think Learning Center and all the things will be much less sensitive.
So while, you know, from a pride perspective, you guys want to do it, I totally get it.
Let's try.
I just started to see how things were unfolding.
I'm just, just no.
Yeah.
We'll see.
No, and I mean, I think from progress, from a progress standpoint, like we can get you guys good results on like the AI and SEO, not content written for humans, which is we have experts to do that.
So we can sort of build the top of funnel for you and get other people like.
Thank
Build awareness, like brand authority, things like that, that are maybe not as worth it to have like a DevRel or your engineers doing.
But still, you know, we want them to be factually accurate within your tone, kind of build that trust as well.
Yeah, for sure.
Yeah, I think the bar is pretty high.
so that's kind of what I'm like trying to figure out how we keep it up.
Well, like, you know, driving volume, which is always not an easy one to do.
Yeah, totally.
So I guess the, then the piece for us to plan is kind of what that learning center looks like.
I'm guessing you'll want it to be separate from the blog, just so that that's a distinguished space.

And so we, you know, that would be, we can submit tickets on our end for design to set up kind of a landing page for the, like a landing page template for the learning center, what that might look like.
And then how.
Have kind of a template for what the blog content would look like within that.

I think we would want, and I'm sure you would want this too, but like that your dev team that manages the website would build out kind of those templates within your CMS, and then we could just push things to that.
Yeah, yeah, and then we probably would want our designer to review and potentially edit, because they're also very, very particular there.
Okay, yeah, I mean, if you guys want to do, like, if your designer wants to take that, we, you know.

Yeah, I mean, I think we probably would be good to take, like, a draft design, basically, and then, Okay, so we'll do V1, and then your designer can, like, finalize it from there, submit it, you can build that space out, and we can start to generate content that once the space is built, we can have content to drop in there that's...
Like Learning Center content, so it will be populated when it launches.
Okay.
Okay, and then the glossary piece, we have the modal glossary as kind of our quality bar.
So we're calibrating that pipeline.
And I just wanted to reshare the content OS link.
I know that Sydney added a few more terms based on the feedback last time that, you know, it's not just LLM inference, it's like general, more general inference terms.
So I think that that should be updated.
And I don't think that like generally the content OS suggestions, assignments, like the keywords and stuff are still things that you'll want to rank for.
And so we'll just be generating content knowing that it will go into that learning center.
Okay.
So I went into the document.
I just don't see the diff.
So I just wasn't sure what was added because there is like ideas for approval versus full list of approved ideas.
So is it in the full list of approved ideas that she added or?
No, would still be in for approval.
So anything that's approved.
Because it's accepted for me.
Yeah, that you've already accepted, it would be the other piece.
But for approval is where she added things.
Yeah, but I don't see anything.
Okay, let me check with her.
And I'll follow up with you there.
I mean, I don't know if you can see because you're probably on your phone.
Oh, yeah.
But I see ideas for approval have nothing.
I just have this list.
Yeah, so.
Okay, interesting.
Yeah.
Yeah, I know, you know, she built like an automation.
So like that pulls from the more populated air table.
So there might just be, they might not, just have not pulled through.
So I'll message her about that.
Okay.
Let me know a follow-up.
I should be less busy now that we can launch.
So yeah, I was definitely running out of time.
Yeah.
Okay.
Great.
And then we have the two drafts from the outlines, just to give you a sense of timeline.
Actually, Stevie, I think Ife just tagged you because the drafts are done.

So they'll go through a Stevie review and a Sydney review for, so Stevie review for like accuracy, technical, on the technical side, and then Sydney, just to make sure that we're adhering to the tone and style and everything.
And then those will go to you.
So I would say that they, you should have versions of those early next week.
Okay, great.

So for the glossary, wanted to go back to that because, you know what Mike was mentioning last week kind of struck me, it's like, you know, like to him, model is so good, like, you know, I think part of him thinks we can't even do better.
And so I just wanted to put it in your head, like, I think we would want to do something different.
I don't know why the glossary is a glossary.
But yeah, I don't know how much time you want to spend on it.
Like, yeah, I mean, yeah, we can find something that we differentiate ourselves.
Well, well, like, obviously, putting all these terms out and ranking.
I'm just wondering, like, if we end up doing the same, that's probably they're probably not going to want to do it because they don't want to copy people.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And Stevie, like, took a look at that and actually had some thoughts of like, it is, you know, it's a very unique, like, tone and style and things like that.
So she kind of had very similar thoughts of, you know, if we do this, it's not this isn't the way that we would want to present.
It anyways, because it would be very, like, we're not copying.
And so, like, we'll, like, we'll continue generating on the learning center, like down the learning center path with the other ideas.
I think the glossary will be good to supplement as we get going, but hear you that we don't want to do it unless it's going to be differentiated and be actually, like, interesting.
Cool.
Okay.
Great.
Yeah, because maybe, like, for us, leaning on the learning center, mostly, it might be fine without coping.
Yeah.
Okay.
Stevie, is there anything else that you wanted to talk about?
I don't know.
I think everything's been covered.
And yeah, we're just constantly refining the pipelines and everything to make sure to reflect your feedback.
And so, um, any and all feedback is always welcome.
And...
Yeah, I'm thinking of, you know, things with the Glossary and the Learning Center, because, yeah, I've been in charge of onboarding and learning centers, putting all that together.
It's not an easy feat to even get adoption, you know, once you have it together, and it is a lot of work to put together.
And so, yeah, we want to make sure that we can help you guys get that right.
Okay, cool.

Yeah, and I would love to see content, because as we're running at the end of our, like, kind of trial period in a couple of weeks, like, you know, it be good to make sure that that's going well, and then we can continue, because we're being kind of like, we're not sure what we want to do yet.
So, I just want to be clear.
Perfect.
Cool.
Okay.
All right, awesome.
Well, I look forward to seeing the articles, and then, yeah, let me know if there is a new, how I can see the new things.
So, that I don't go through the list again.
Yeah, will do.
Um, yeah, I can get you an answer, uh, for that this afternoon.
Awesome.
Cool.
All right.
Well, enjoy Halloween.
Happy Halloween.
Halloween.
Take care.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content strategy, review progress, and plan next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Strategy:** Shifted focus to a "Learning Center" for AI/SEO content, separating it from the high-bar blog to protect brand voice while still driving top-of-funnel awareness.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [**Content Pipeline:** The first two article drafts are complete and will be delivered to Baseten early next week, following internal technical (Stevie) and tone (Sydney) reviews.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Glossary Pivot:** The glossary project is paused. Baseten wants a differentiated product, not a copy of the Modal glossary, and will prioritize the Learning Center instead.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [**Content OS Sync:** New glossary terms added by Sydney are not visible to Marylise in the "Ideas for Approval" tab. Andi will investigate this automation issue.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)

## Topics

### Content Strategy Pivot

  - [**Problem:** Baseten's blog has a high quality bar, making it difficult for external teams to match the brand's voice and tone.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
  - [**Solution:** Create a separate "Learning Center" for AI/SEO-driven content.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=204.0)
      - [**Rationale:** This protects the blog's brand integrity while allowing GrowthX to build top-of-funnel awareness and authority.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [**Glossary Pivot:**](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
      - [**Decision:** Pause the glossary project.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Baseten wants a differentiated product, not a copy of the Modal glossary, and will prioritize the Learning Center.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)

### Learning Center Implementation

  - [**Goal:** Launch the Learning Center pre-populated with content.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=397.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
    1.  [**GrowthX:** Create a V1 landing page design template.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
    2.  [**Baseten:** Finalize the design via their internal designer.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
    3.  [**Baseten:** Build the template in their CMS.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)
    4.  [**GrowthX:** Push content to the new templates.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)

### Content Pipeline & Review

  - [**Status:** The first two article drafts are complete.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Internal Review Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
      - [**Technical Accuracy:** Stevie (GrowthX)](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
      - [**Tone & Style:** Sydney (GrowthX)](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Delivery:** Drafts will be sent to Marylise (Baseten) early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**Urgency:** Baseten's trial period ends in a few weeks; seeing the drafts is critical for evaluating the partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=747.0)

### Content OS Sync Issue

  - [**Problem:** New glossary terms added by Sydney are not visible to Marylise in the "Ideas for Approval" tab of the Content OS document.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [**Cause:** Likely an automation failure in the pipeline that pulls data from Airtable.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=536.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Andi:** Investigate the Content OS sync issue with Sydney and update Marylise today.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=426.0)
  - [**Andi:** Deliver the first two article drafts to Marylise early next week.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=565.0)
  - [**GrowthX Team:** Create a V1 design template for the Learning Center landing page.](https://fathom.video/share/a-KN722xFQh6kxFEXwt2BZL2Gk-tWiSW?tab=summary&timestamp=329.0)

action_items.description: Submit V1 Learning Center landing page + article templates to Marylise; then BaseTen designer reviews, dev builds CMS templates
Message Sydney re: Content OS 'For approval' visibility; then update Marylise
Review 2 article drafts for accuracy; then send to Sydney for tone/style
